risks: Public network access is enabled on both Service Bus namespace and Cosmos DB account — no network isolation is configured, leaving data-plane endpoints exposed to the internet.
ACA environment has no VNet integration — WAF recommends deploying Container Apps environments into a virtual network for network segmentation and egress control.
Container app processor has external ingress enabled but functions as an event-driven consumer via Dapr pub/sub — this exposes an unnecessary public HTTP endpoint and increases the attack surface.

No Azure Key Vault resource is included for secrets management — best practices recommend storing sensitive configuration (e.g., connection strings, keys) in Key Vault rather than in app configuration.
Zone redundancy is disabled on the ACA environment (zoneRedundant: false) — a single availability zone failure could impact the processing pipeline.
No explicit RBAC role assignment resources are defined for the container app's managed identity to access Service Bus and Cosmos DB data planes.
correctionsRecommended: Add VNet integration for the ACA environment and switch the container app processor ingress from external to internal, since it consumes events via Dapr rather than inbound HTTP traffic.
Configure private endpoints or disable public network access on the Service Bus namespace and Cosmos DB account to restrict data-plane access to the virtual network.
Add an Azure Key Vault resource (Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults) and reference it for secrets management in the container app, replacing any inline secret configuration.

Add Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments resources to grant the container app's system-assigned managed identity the 'Azure Service Bus Data Receiver' role on the Service Bus subscription and the 'Cosmos DB Built-in Data Contributor' role on the Cosmos DB account.
Consider enabling zone redundancy (zoneRedundant: true) on the ACA environment for production-grade reliability.
Consider switching the container app to a user-assigned managed identity to enable pre-authorization and identity reuse across resources, as recommended by ACA IaC deployment rules.
